message.content: I'm extending a Microsoft Graph profile-photo feature so that COMMENT AUTHOR avatars and the ADMIN USER LIST show users' M365 photos. The backend already has a helper to compute an effective avatar. I need a precise map across two repos. Report concrete file paths, function names, line numbers, and short code excerpts. Do NOT edit anything — investigation only. ## Backend: C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend (Node/Express, JavaScript) 1. **Comments**: Find where comments are created and fetched. Search controllers/services/routes for "comment" (e.g. controllers/*comment*, services/*comment*, routes/*comment*, and grep for "comment" in notification.* and rfq.* and item* controllers). For the endpoint(s) that RETURN comments: what author fields are included per comment (author user id? name? email? profile_photo)? How is the author joined (Sequelize include of users? a stored author name string)? Show the query/shape returned. 2. **Admin user list**: The frontend route /apps/admin/users (manage_users) must fetch a list of users. Find the backend endpoint that returns all users for admin management. Search routes/ for user-list endpoints (user.router.js, admin.router.js, role.router.js), and controllers/services. What fields per user are returned (id, name, email, level/role, status, profile_photo)? Show the query. 3. Confirm the existing helper `services/graph-photo.service.js` exports `effectivePhoto(user)`, `backfillStale(users, max)`, `syncStaffPhoto(user)` and note its require path from the comment + admin-user controllers/services. ## Frontend: C:\repos\dflow\designflow-frontend (Angular) 4. **Comment component**: Find the component(s) that render comments (search for "comment" in src/app — *.component.ts/html, likely under itemDetail, rfq, or a shared comments component, and anything tied to the notification/mention system). Does each comment show an AUTHOR AVATAR? If so, what field/URL does the bind to, and what's the fallback (initials/icon)? If there is NO avatar shown currently, note that and describe where one could be added (the author name element). Quote the relevant HTML. 5. **Admin users page**: Find the manage_users page component (src/app/pages/manage_users/ per earlier findings). Does the user list/table show an avatar column? What field does it bind to? Quote the relevant HTML and the TS that loads the users (which service method / endpoint). 6. For both, identify the service method (in main.service.ts or a dedicated service) that calls the backend comments endpoint and the admin-users endpoint, and the shape of the data the component consumes (so I know what field to populate with the effective photo). Give me enough that I can wire the effective photo into both the comment-author avatar and the admin user list with minimal, correct edits.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The `user.router.js` only has `/me` and `/photo` — no admin user-list. Let me find where the admin user list endpoint lives. Let me check admin router/controller/service and grep for the manage_users endpoint usage, plus look at the frontend comment and manage_users components.

content.content:
1	const express = require('express');
2	const router = express.Router();
3	const { authRole } = require("../helpers/auth");
4	const adminController = require('../controllers/admin.controller');
5	const Multer = require('multer');
6	
7	// In-memory storage for PDF upload — never touches disk
8	const pdfUpload = Multer({ storage: Multer.memoryStorage(), limits: { fileSize: 50 * 1024 * 1024 } });
9	
10	// CSV upload (Section 301). The USTR all-lists CSV is ~3 MB; 10 MB headroom covers
11	// future growth and lets admins upload XLSX-converted CSVs without truncation.
12	const csvUpload = Multer({ storage: Multer.memoryStorage(), limits: { fileSize: 10 * 1024 * 1024 } });
13	
14	/* ====================================================================================
15	   📁 ART TYPES - Routes
16	   ==================================================================================== */
17	
18	// 🔍 Get all active Art Types
19	router.get('/getArtTypes', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.getArtTypes);
20	
21	// ➕ Create a new Art Type
22	router.post('/createArtType', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.createArtType);
23	
24	// ✏️ Update an existing Art Type
25	router.patch('/updateArtType/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.updateArtType);
26	
27	// ❌ Soft-delete (deactivate) an Art Type
28	router.delete('/deleteArtType/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.deleteArtType);
29	
30	/* ====================================================================================
31	   🎨 ARTIST TYPES - Routes
32	   ==================================================================================== */
33	
34	// 🔍 Get all active Artist Types
35	router.get('/getArtistTypes', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.getArtistTypes);
36	
37	// ➕ Create a new Artist Type
38	router.post('/createArtistType', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.createArtistType);
39	
40	// ✏️ Update an existing Artist Type
41	router.patch('/updateArtistType/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.updateArtistType);
42	
43	// ❌ Soft-delete (deactivate) an Artist Type
44	router.delete('/deleteArtistType/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.deleteArtistType);
45	
46	/* ====================================================================================
47	   🎨 ARTISTS - Routes
48	   ==================================================================================== */
49	
50	// 🔍 Get all active Artists
51	router.get('/getArtists', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.getArtists);
52	
53	// ➕ Create a new Artist
54	router.post('/createArtist', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.createArtist);
55	
56	// ✏️ Update an existing Artist
57	router.patch('/updateArtist/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.updateArtist);
58	
59	// ❌ Soft-delete (deactivate) an Artist
60	router.delete('/deleteArtist/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.deleteArtist);
61	
62	/* ====================================================================================
63	   👶 AGE GROUP - Routes
64	   ==================================================================================== */
65	
66	
67	// 🔍 Get all active Age Groups
68	router.get('/getAgeGroups', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.getAgeGroups);
69	
70	// ➕ Create a new Age Group
71	router.post('/createAgeGroup', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.createAgeGroup);
72	
73	// ✏️ Update an existing Age Group
74	router.patch('/updateAgeGroup/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.updateAgeGroup);
75	
76	// ❌ Soft-delete (deactivate) an Age Group
77	router.delete('/deleteAgeGroup/:id', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.deleteAgeGroup);
78	
79	
80	/* ====================================================================================
81	   ⬇️ ADD ROUTES FOR OTHER TABLES BELOW THIS LINE
82	   ==================================================================================== */
83	   
84	// 🔍 Get list of merch group categories
85	router.get('/getMerchGroupCategories/', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.getMerchGroupCategories);
86	// ✏️ Update an existing Age Group
87	router.patch('/updateMerchGroup/', authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'sales', 'production', 'admin']), adminController.updateMerchGroup);
88	
89	/* ====================================================================================
90	   ⚙️  APP SETTINGS - Routes (admin only)
91	   ==================================================================================== */
92	
93	// 🔍 Get a single app setting by key
94	router.get('/app-settings/:key', authRole(['admin']), adminController.getAppSetting);
95	
96	// 💾 Create or update an app setting by key
97	router.put('/app-settings/:key', authRole(['admin']), adminController.setAppSetting);
98	
99	// 📈 Aggregate AI prompt/context cache telemetry
100	router.get('/ai-cache-stats', authRole(['admin']), adminController.getAiCacheStats);
101	
102	/* ====================================================================================
103	   📋  SECTION 301 - Routes (admin only)
104	   ==================================================================================== */
105	
106	// 📤 Upload Section 301 PDF and extract HTS tariff data (field name: "pdf")
107	router.post('/section301/upload', authRole(['admin']), pdfUpload.single('pdf'), adminController.uploadSection301);
108	
109	// 📤 Upload Section 301 CSV (preferred over PDF — column-addressed, no regex mis-assignment).
110	//    Field name: "csv". See helpers/section301-csv.js for the expected shape.
111	router.post('/section301/upload-csv', authRole(['admin']), csvUpload.single('csv'), adminController.uploadSection301Csv);
112	
113	// 📊 Get summary of currently stored Section 301 data (code count + lists, no full JSON)
114	router.get('/section301/status', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager']), adminController.getSection301Status);
115	
116	// 🔍 Lookup a single HTS code in the Section 301 tariff data
117	router.get('/section301/lookup', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager']), adminController.lookupSection301);
118	
119	/* ====================================================================================
120	   📦  HTS MFN RATES - Routes (bundled USITC data)
121	   ==================================================================================== */
122	
123	// 📊 Status of bundled HTS rates lookup
124	router.get('/hts-rates/status', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'designer']), adminController.getHtsRatesStatus);
125	
126	// 🔍 Look up MFN general rate for a single HTS code
127	router.get('/hts-rates/lookup', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'designer']), adminController.lookupHtsRate);
128	
129	/* ====================================================================================
130	   🇺🇸 TRUMP 2025 RECIPROCAL TARIFFS - Routes
131	   ==================================================================================== */
132	
133	// 📋 List all configured country rates
134	router.get('/trump2025/list', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'designer']), adminController.getTrump2025List);
135	
136	// 💾 Replace the full country → rate list (admin only)
137	router.put('/trump2025/list', authRole(['admin']), adminController.saveTrump2025List);
138	
139	// 🔍 Look up the rate for a single country
140	router.get('/trump2025/lookup', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'designer']), adminController.lookupTrump2025);
141	
142	/* ====================================================================================
143	   🏭 FACTORIES - Routes (admin / sourcing manager)
144	   ==================================================================================== */
145	
146	// 📋 Full factory list with country (admin UI)
147	router.get('/factories', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager']), adminController.listFactories);
148	
149	// 🌍 Compact factory_nickname → factory_country map (used by the RFQ sub-grid)
150	router.get('/factories/countries', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager', 'designer', 'sales', 'production', 'vendor']), adminController.getFactoryCountryMap);
151	
152	// ✏️ Update a single factory's country
153	router.patch('/factories/:id', authRole(['admin', 'sourcing_manager']), adminController.updateFactoryCountry);
154	
155	/* ====================================================================================
156	   🎭  IMPERSONATION - Routes (admin only)
157	   ==================================================================================== */
158	
159	// 🎭 Issue an impersonation token for the given user (admin only)
160	router.post('/impersonate/:userId', authRole(['admin']), adminController.startImpersonation);
161	
162	module.exports = router;

content.text: Let me read `mapUser` in utility.js to confirm the exact per-user output shape.

I now have the complete picture. The `mapUser` output keys are dynamic (driven by GridLayout `field` names like "User ID #", "Email", etc.) and currently emits only id, first/last name, status, email, role — no photo. Let me verify the comment route prefix to give an accurate endpoint path.

I have everything needed. Here is the complete map. # Microsoft Graph Photo Wiring Map — Comments + Admin User List ## The shared helper (already exists, confirmed) `C:\repos\dflow\designflow-backend\services\graph-photo.service.js` - Exports (line 108): `{ syncStaffPhoto, backfillStale, effectivePhoto, isFresh, SYNC_TTL_MS }` - `effectivePhoto(user)` (lines 33-36): returns `user.profile_photo || user.graph_photo || null`. Pure, no DB/network — safe to call on any user row that has those two columns. - `backfillStale(users, max=10)` (lines 100-106): fire-and-forget bulk Graph fetch for staff with no `profile_photo` and stale `graph_photo_synced_at`. - `syncStaffPhoto(user)` (lines 49-92): single-user throttled fetch+cache, returns URL. - Already required and used in `services/user.service.js` line 3: `const GraphPhotoService = require('./graph-photo.service');` — so the require path from `services/*` is `./graph-photo.service`. The `users` model (`models\db\users.js`) has the needed columns: `profile_photo` (TEXT, line 51), `graph_photo` (TEXT, line 55), `graph_photo_synced_at` (DATE, line 59). --- ## BACKEND PART 1 — Comments **Route:** `routes\comment.router.js` line 9 — `GET /getComments/:id` → `CommentController.getComment`. (Mount prefix is `/api/comment`, per frontend call `/api/comment/getComments/{id}`.) **Controller:** `controllers\comment.controller.js` lines 6-9 — `getComment` just delegates to `CommentService.getComments(req.params.id)` and returns the raw rows. **Service (the query to change):** `services\comment.service.js` lines 7-24, `CommentService.getComments`. The author join is a Sequelize include of `sql.users` aliased `'users'` with: ```js attributes: ['id', 'name', 'email'], // line 17 — NO photo fields ``` So each returned comment carries `users: [{ id, name, email }]` (array; note it's `findAll`-style include returning an array per comment). **To expose the effective photo**, add `profile_photo`, `graph_photo`, `graph_photo_synced_at` to that `attributes` list, then either map `effectivePhoto(comment.users[0])` into a new field server-side, or call `effectivePhoto` per row in the controller. `backfillStale` can be fired on the joined user rows here too. **Model:** `models\db\comments.js` — comments table has `user_id` (FK), no denormalized author name. Author identity comes entirely from the include. --- ## BACKEND PART 2 — Admin user list Not in `admin.router.js`. The real endpoint: **Route:** `routes\lib.router.js` line 179 — `POST /getAllUsers`, `authRole(['admin','sourcing_manager'])` → `customers.getAllUsers` (`controllers/main.controller.js`). **Controller:** `controllers\main.controller.js` lines 579-584 — delegates to `Customer.getAllUsers(req, cb)`. **Model (the query):** `models\lib.model.js` line 3346 `Customer.getAllUsers`. For the normal user view (`tableName='users'`, lines 3402, 3429-3431) it does `sql.users.findAll({ where: condition, order:[['id','ASC']] })` (no `attributes` restriction — so full rows including `profile_photo`/`graph_photo` are loaded), then reshapes via `utility.mapUser(colHeaders, data)` at line 3443. **The reshaper (critical):** `helpers\utility.js` lines 343-352, `mapUser`. It builds each row with **dynamic keys** from GridLayout `field` names and emits ONLY: ```js { [id]: ...id, [first_name]: ...name, [last_name]: ...lastname, [status]: ...status, [email]: ...email, [role]: ...level } ``` No photo is passed through. **To expose the photo**, add a key here, e.g. `pic: GraphPhotoService.effectivePhoto(vendor.dataValues)` (the param is misnamed `vendor` but is a users row). Source rows already contain `profile_photo`/`graph_photo` since no `attributes` filter is applied. You can also call `backfillStale(data, ...)` in `getAllUsers` before mapping. --- ## FRONTEND PART 1 — Comments (NO avatar currently) **Component:** `src\app\pages\comments\comment\comment.component.ts` + `.html`. The comment HTML has a **hardcoded placeholder image**, not a per-author avatar: `src\app\pages\comments\comment\comment.component.html` lines 2-4: ```html
``` Author name is rendered line 8: `
{{ comment.user_name }}
`. **To wire the photo**, bind that `` to a new field (e.g. `comment.author_photo`) with the static icon as fallback. **Interface:** `src\app\pages\comments\types\commentInterface.ts` — `CommentInterface` (lines 1-13) has `user_id`, `user_name`, but **no photo field**; add one (e.g. `author_photo?: string`). **Data mapping (where to populate):** `src\app\pages\comments\comments\comments.component.ts` lines 38-58, `getComments()`. It maps the backend rows; line 46 already reads the join: `user_name: item.users.length > 0 ? item.users[0].name : ''`. Add `author_photo: item.users[0]?.` here (or read `item.users[0].profile_photo || item.users[0].graph_photo`). **Service method:** `src\app\helpers\services\main.service.ts` lines 314-316: ```ts getComments(id) { return this.http.get(`${serverAddress}/api/comment/getComments/${id}`, ...) } ``` --- ## FRONTEND PART 2 — Admin users page **Component:** `src\app\pages\manage_users\manage_users.component.ts` (selector `manage_users`). **Loader:** `loadUsersView()` lines 439-490. It calls `this.itemService.getAllUsers({...})` (lines 444-448) and builds columns from `USER_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG`. Notably, lines 458-465 **already special-case a photo column**: ```ts colHeaders.forEach(col => { if (col.col_id === 'pic') { col.cellRenderer = 'imageEditor'; col.cellStyle = params => params.value ? { 'text-align': '-webkit-center' } : null; col.editable = false; } }); ``` …but the column config does **not actually define a `pic` column** yet. **Column config:** `src\app\helpers\ag-grid\user.grid.config.ts` lines 34-46 — columns are only: `id`, `last_name`, `first_name`, `email`, `role`, `status`. **No `pic` column.** So to show an avatar you must (a) add `addColumn('Pic','pic', width)` here so the existing `imageEditor` renderer branch fires, and (b) have the backend `mapUser` emit a value under that column's `field` name. There is an existing `imageEditor` AG Grid renderer (referenced by name) you can reuse. **Service method:** `src\app\helpers\services\main.service.ts` lines 287-289: ```ts getAllUsers(data) { return this.http.post(`${serverAddress}/getAllUsers`, data, ...) } ``` The grid rows are dynamic objects keyed by GridLayout field names (from `mapUser`), consumed directly as AG Grid `rowData` (line 585). Whatever field name the `pic` column maps to is what `mapUser` must populate with `effectivePhoto(...)`. --- ## Minimal edit summary (for your implementation pass) | Area | File | Change | |---|---|---| | Comments backend | `services\comment.service.js` L17 | add `profile_photo, graph_photo, graph_photo_synced_at` to include attributes; map `effectivePhoto` into each row (and optionally `backfillStale`) | | Comments require | same | `const GraphPhotoService = require('./graph-photo.service')` | | Comments FE map | `comments\comments\comments.component.ts` L46 | add `author_photo: item.users[0]?....` | | Comments FE iface | `comments\types\commentInterface.ts` | add `author_photo?: string` | | Comments FE view | `comments\comment\comment.component.html` L3 | bind `` | | Admin backend | `helpers\utility.js` `mapUser` L344-351 | add photo key = `effectivePhoto(row.dataValues)` | | Admin backend | `models\lib.model.js` `getAllUsers` ~L3429 | optionally `backfillStale(data)` before mapping | | Admin FE col | `helpers\ag-grid\user.grid.config.ts` L34-46 | add a `pic` column (the `imageEditor` wiring at `manage_users.component.ts` L459 already keys off `col_id === 'pic'`) | Backend user rows already load the photo columns (no `attributes` restriction in `getAllUsers`); comments do not (explicit `attributes` list), so the include must be widened there.
